name: Galle Fort
description: A historic UNESCO World Heritage Site with cobbled streets, ocean views, and centuries of history.

highlights.name: Galle Clock Tower
highlights.description: An iconic 19th-century clock tower that dominates the Fort skyline.

highlights.name: Galle Lighthouse
highlights.description: Sri Lanka's oldest light station, built in 1848, offering panoramic sea views.

highlights.name: Dutch Hospital Shopping Precinct
highlights.description: Historic hospital now housing boutique shops, restaurants, and cafes.

highlights.name: Ramparts Walk
highlights.description: Scenic walkway atop the fort walls with sunset views and sea breeze.

highlights.name: Flag Rock Bastion
highlights.description: Famous for dramatic waves and sunset, the best spot to watch cliff jumpers.

highlights.name: Galle Fort Walls
highlights.description: Walkable 17th-century walls that once protected the city from invaders.

highlights.name: Old Gate
highlights.description: Original entrance with British and Dutch coats of arms carved in stone.

highlights.name: Maritime Archaeology Museum
highlights.description: Museum with exhibits from shipwrecks and colonial era.

highlights.name: Meera Mosque
highlights.description: A white-washed mosque built in 1904, blending Islamic and colonial architecture.

highlights.name: All Saints’ Church
highlights.description: Gothic-style Anglican church with beautiful stained glass windows.

highlights.name: National Maritime Museum
highlights.description: Museum featuring local marine history, fishing, and ecology exhibits.

highlights.name: Pedlar Street
highlights.description: Charming street lined with art galleries, cafes, and boutiques.

highlights.name: Historical Mansion Museum
highlights.description: Private museum with antiques, curios, and colonial artifacts.

highlights.name: Bastion Museum
highlights.description: Exhibition dedicated to Galle’s defense and colonial history.

highlights.name: Groote Kerk (Great Church)
highlights.description: Historic Dutch Reformed Church with a centuries-old organ and tombstones.

events.title: Galle Literary Festival
events.description: Annual celebration of literature, arts, and culture within the fort.

name: Dutch Reformed Church
description: Built in 1755, this beautiful church showcases Dutch colonial architecture with ornate pulpit and historic gravestones.

highlights.name: Historic Organ
highlights.description: Original Dutch pipe organ from the 18th century.

highlights.name: Colonial Gravestones
highlights.description: Ancient tombstones with Dutch inscriptions in the church floor.

events.title: Christmas Service
events.description: Traditional Christmas service with organ music.

name: All Saints’ Church
description: Gothic-style Anglican church with beautiful stained glass windows and historic charm.

name: Meera Mosque
description: A white-washed mosque built in 1904, blending Islamic and colonial architecture.

name: Maritime Archaeology Museum
description: Museum with exhibits from shipwrecks and colonial era.

name: National Maritime Museum
description: Museum featuring local marine history, fishing, and ecology exhibits.

name: Historical Mansion Museum
description: Private museum with antiques, curios, and colonial artifacts.

name: Yala National Park Entrance
description: Sri Lanka’s most famous national park, home to leopards, elephants, sloth bears, and an array of birdlife.

highlights.name: Leopard Watching
highlights.description: Yala boasts one of the highest leopard densities in the world.

highlights.name: Elephant Herds
highlights.description: Large herds of wild elephants roam freely in the park.

highlights.name: Magul Maha Viharaya
highlights.description: Ancient Buddhist ruins inside the park, surrounded by nature.

events.title: Bird Watching Festival
events.description: A seasonal event for bird enthusiasts to spot rare species.

name: Udawalawe National Park
description: Best known for its elephant herds and birdlife, Udawalawe is a prime spot for wildlife photography.

highlights.name: Elephant Transit Home
highlights.description: See orphaned elephant calves being cared for and released to the wild.

highlights.name: Safari Jeep Tours
highlights.description: Guided tours for wildlife spotting, best at sunrise and sunset.

name: Bundala National Park
description: A Ramsar wetland, Bundala is famous for migratory birds, crocodiles, and wild buffalo.

highlights.name: Flamingo Watching
highlights.description: See thousands of migratory flamingos (Nov–Mar).

highlights.name: Saltwater Crocodiles
highlights.description: Watch crocodiles basking along the lagoons.

name: Wilpattu National Park
description: Sri Lanka's largest national park, famous for its lakes (villus), leopards, sloth bears, and dense forest.

highlights.name: Leopard Safari
highlights.description: Wilpattu is renowned for leopard sightings in thick forest and near waterholes.

highlights.name: Willus (Natural Lakes)
highlights.description: Picturesque lakes attract elephants, deer, and diverse birdlife.

highlights.name: Sloth Bear Watching
highlights.description: One of the best places in Sri Lanka to spot the elusive sloth bear.

events.title: Wilpattu Birding Week
events.description: A guided week for birdwatchers and nature lovers.

name: Minneriya National Park
description: Famous for 'The Gathering,' where hundreds of wild elephants congregate around the Minneriya Tank during the dry season.

highlights.name: The Gathering
highlights.description: One of the largest wild Asian elephant gatherings in the world (July–September).

highlights.name: Minneriya Tank
highlights.description: Ancient reservoir attracting herds of elephants and water birds.

events.title: Elephant Conservation Workshop
events.description: A special event for conservationists, travelers, and wildlife photographers.

name: Kaudulla National Park
description: A popular eco-tourism destination with large herds of elephants and migratory birds, centered around the Kaudulla Tank.

highlights.name: Elephant Watching
highlights.description: Hundreds of elephants migrate between Kaudulla, Minneriya, and Wasgamuwa parks.

highlights.name: Kaudulla Tank
highlights.description: Picturesque ancient reservoir attracting wildlife, especially in the dry season.

events.title: Kaudulla Bird Migration Day
events.description: Spot rare migratory birds with local guides.

name: Sacred City of Anuradhapura
description: The ancient capital of Sri Lanka and UNESCO World Heritage Site, home to towering dagobas, sacred Bodhi trees, ancient monasteries, and centuries of Buddhist history.

highlights.name: Sri Maha Bodhi
highlights.description: The oldest historically documented tree in the world, grown from a cutting of the original Bodhi tree under which the Buddha attained enlightenment.

highlights.name: Ruwanwelisaya Stupa
highlights.description: A massive white dagoba built in 140 B.C., an iconic landmark of Anuradhapura.

highlights.name: Jetavanaramaya Stupa
highlights.description: Once the third tallest structure in the ancient world, made of over 90 million bricks.

highlights.name: Thuparamaya Dagoba
highlights.description: The first dagoba built after the introduction of Buddhism to Sri Lanka, housing a collarbone relic of the Buddha.

highlights.name: Abhayagiri Monastery
highlights.description: A vast monastic complex with ruins, moonstones, and forested trails.

events.title: Poson Full Moon Festival
events.description: Thousands of pilgrims gather to celebrate the introduction of Buddhism to Sri Lanka with processions, lanterns, and religious rituals.

name: Ancient City of Polonnaruwa
description: Sri Lanka’s second most ancient kingdom, Polonnaruwa is a UNESCO World Heritage Site featuring well-preserved ruins, temples, stupas, and beautiful stone sculptures.

highlights.name: Gal Vihara
highlights.description: Four massive Buddha statues carved into a single granite rock, a masterpiece of Sinhalese sculpture.

highlights.name: Royal Palace of Parakramabahu
highlights.description: Ruins of a grand 12th-century palace complex with thick walls and impressive brickwork.

highlights.name: Vatadage
highlights.description: A circular relic house with stone carvings and guard stones, considered one of the best-preserved monuments.

highlights.name: Rankoth Vehera
highlights.description: A massive brick stupa surrounded by a monastery, the largest dagoba in Polonnaruwa.

highlights.name: Lankathilaka Image House
highlights.description: Imposing 17-meter-tall standing Buddha statue within a large brick shrine.

highlights.name: Nissanka Latha Mandapaya
highlights.description: A unique stone pavilion with carved lotus columns, believed to be used for chanting Buddhist scriptures.

events.title: Polonnaruwa Perahera
events.description: A traditional religious procession with dancers, elephants, and music, celebrating the ancient heritage of the city.

name: Pigeon Island National Park
description: One of Sri Lanka’s two marine national parks, renowned for its powdery white sand, turquoise water, and some of the best snorkeling with coral reefs and turtles.

highlights.name: Coral Gardens
highlights.description: Colorful shallow reefs teeming with tropical fish and live coral.

highlights.name: Snorkeling with Turtles
highlights.description: Swim alongside hawksbill and green turtles in crystal-clear waters.

highlights.name: Pigeon Colony
highlights.description: Home to the endangered rock pigeon, which gives the island its name.

events.title: Pigeon Island Marine Conservation Day
events.description: A day of guided snorkeling, beach cleanups, and conservation talks.

name: Koneswaram Temple
description: A magnificent Hindu temple perched atop Swami Rock with panoramic views of the Indian Ocean.

highlights.name: Swami Rock
highlights.description: Sheer cliff offering breathtaking views and known as Lovers’ Leap.

highlights.name: Sacred Pond
highlights.description: A holy water tank within the temple complex.

events.title: Ther Festival
events.description: Annual chariot procession drawing thousands of pilgrims.

name: Nilaveli Beach
description: A pristine stretch of white sand and turquoise water, famous for its calm seas and ideal swimming and water sports.

highlights.name: Sunrise Walks
highlights.description: Peaceful early morning walks along the empty shoreline.

name: Marble Beach
description: A tranquil cove with crystal-clear water and fine sand, known for its safe swimming and picnicking.

highlights.name: Swimming Area
highlights.description: Safe area for families and kids to swim.

name: Fort Frederick
description: A colonial-era fort originally built by the Portuguese and later fortified by the Dutch and British. Offers panoramic views and wild spotted deer.

highlights.name: Deer Watching
highlights.description: Spotted deer roam freely inside the fort.

name: Arugam Bay Beach
description: World-famous surf beach with laid-back vibes, ideal for surfing, swimming, and beach parties.

highlights.name: Main Point
highlights.description: Popular surf break attracting surfers from around the globe.

highlights.name: Whiskey Point
highlights.description: Chilled spot for sunrise surfing and social gatherings.

name: Peanut Farm Beach
description: A serene beach popular for beginner and intermediate surfers, with rustic beach shacks and laid-back vibes.

highlights.name: Surf Breaks
highlights.description: Gentle waves ideal for beginner and intermediate surfers.

highlights.name: Rustic Beach Cafés
highlights.description: Simple beach shacks serving fresh coconut and snacks.

name: Elephant Rock
description: A large rocky outcrop south of Arugam Bay, popular for sunrise hikes, panoramic views, and a scenic surf break below.

highlights.name: Scenic Viewpoint
highlights.description: Panoramic views over the ocean and bay, best at sunrise.

highlights.name: Surf Point
highlights.description: A less-crowded surf break for intermediate surfers.

name: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Sigiriya is an ancient rock fortress famed for its frescoes, water gardens, and the Lion's Paw entrance. Once a royal palace, it is a marvel of urban planning and ancient engineering.

highlights.name: Lion's Paw Terrace
highlights.description: Giant stone lion paws guard the main staircase to the summit.

highlights.name: Frescoes & Mirror Wall
highlights.description: Exquisite ancient frescoes of celestial maidens and a wall once polished to a mirror finish, inscribed with ancient graffiti.

highlights.name: Summit Palace Ruins
highlights.description: Ruins of King Kasyapa's palace and panoramic views over the jungle.

highlights.name: Water Gardens
highlights.description: Symmetrical pools, fountains, and landscaped terraces at the base, showcasing ancient hydraulic engineering.

highlights.name: Boulder Gardens
highlights.description: Large boulders and winding paths, once used as meditation spaces and shelters.

highlights.name: Cobra Hood Cave
highlights.description: A rock formation resembling a cobra’s hood, adorned with ancient paintings.

highlights.name: Audience Hall
highlights.description: An open stone platform where the king met his ministers.

highlights.name: Terraced Gardens
highlights.description: Stepped terraces rising from the gardens to the base of the rock.

name: Pidurangala Rock
description: A massive rock formation offering the best panoramic view of Sigiriya and stunning sunrises. A short hike leads to an ancient temple and reclining Buddha.

highlights.name: Summit View
highlights.description: Unbeatable sunrise/sunset views of Sigiriya and the surrounding forest.

highlights.name: Reclining Buddha Statue
highlights.description: Ancient brick Buddha statue partway up the climb.

name: Nine Arches Bridge
description: A spectacular stone railway viaduct with nine arches, nestled in lush tea fields. A must-see photo spot and popular train-watching viewpoint.

highlights.name: Viewpoint Trail
highlights.description: Scenic walking trail leading to the best photo spot.

name: Little Adam's Peak
description: A short, rewarding hike with stunning 360° views over Ella Gap, tea plantations, and rolling hills. Best at sunrise or sunset.

highlights.name: Summit Views
highlights.description: Panoramic view of Ella and surrounding mountains.

name: Ella Rock
description: A challenging trek up through tea estates and forest, offering spectacular views over the valley. Popular with sunrise hikers.

highlights.name: Summit Sunrise
highlights.description: Unforgettable sunrise from the rocky outcrop.

name: Ravana Falls
description: A spectacular 25-meter-high waterfall cascading over jagged rocks, easily accessible from Ella town.

highlights.name: Swimming Pool
highlights.description: Natural pool at the base for cooling off (check safety).

name: Demodara Loop
description: Ingenious railway loop and spiral tunnel where the track circles under itself—a marvel of colonial engineering.

highlights.name: Spiral Tunnel View
highlights.description: Watch the train complete the spiral from the station viewpoint.

name: Diyaluma Falls
description: The second tallest waterfall in Sri Lanka, cascading 220 meters in a single stunning drop, surrounded by lush greenery and dramatic cliffs.

highlights.name: Base Viewpoint
highlights.description: View the dramatic full drop from the bottom of the falls.

name: Upper Diyaluma Falls
description: Stunning natural pools at the top of Diyaluma Falls, ideal for a refreshing swim with breathtaking views over the valley below.

highlights.name: Infinity Pools
highlights.description: Natural pools overlooking the edge, perfect for swimming and photography.

name: Temple of the Sacred Tooth Relic
description: Sri Lanka's most sacred Buddhist temple, housing the relic of the tooth of the Buddha, and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

highlights.name: Daily Pooja Ceremonies
highlights.description: Traditional rituals performed three times a day, featuring drummers and dancers.

events.title: Esala Perahera
events.description: One of Asia’s grandest Buddhist festivals with parades, elephants, and dancers.

name: Kandy Lake
description: A picturesque lake at the heart of Kandy, perfect for peaceful strolls and boat rides.

highlights.name: Lake Walk
highlights.description: Shaded lakeside promenade with city and temple views.

name: Royal Botanical Gardens, Peradeniya
description: Famous for its vast collection of orchids, palms, and rare plants. Ideal for nature walks and picnics.

highlights.name: Giant Javan Fig Tree
highlights.description: A majestic tree with enormous roots and canopy.

name: Bahirawakanda Buddha Statue
description: A giant white Buddha statue overlooking Kandy town, offering panoramic views of the city and surrounding hills.

highlights.name: Hilltop Viewpoint
highlights.description: Best spot for sunrise and sunset photos over Kandy.

name: Udawatta Kele Sanctuary
description: A lush forest reserve above Kandy Lake, popular for bird watching, nature walks, and ancient hermitages.

highlights.name: Lover’s Walk Trail
highlights.description: A peaceful forest path with a chance to spot rare birds and monkeys.

name: Unawatuna Beach
description: A crescent-shaped golden sand beach famous for safe swimming, vibrant cafes, snorkeling, and lively nightlife.

name: Jungle Beach
description: A secluded cove with clear turquoise water, surrounded by forest—great for snorkeling and kayaking.

name: Dalawella Beach
description: Famous for its Insta-worthy palm swing, relaxed vibe, turtle spotting, and safe swimming in a natural lagoon.

name: Wijaya Beach
description: A lively beach with famous seafood restaurant, protected lagoon for swimming, and frequent turtle sightings.

highlights.name: Turtle Feeding
highlights.description: Watch and feed wild sea turtles that visit the shallow lagoon—especially around sunset.

name: Hikkaduwa Beach
description: A vibrant beach town famous for surfing, snorkeling with turtles, nightlife, and coral sanctuary.

highlights.name: Coral Sanctuary
highlights.description: Snorkel among colorful corals and tropical fish in the protected marine park.

highlights.name: Turtle Spotting
highlights.description: See giant sea turtles up close, often swimming near the shore.

highlights.name: Surfing
highlights.description: Hikkaduwa is famous for consistent waves and beginner-friendly surf schools.

name: Ahangama Beach
description: Popular among surfers for its consistent waves, sandy stretches, and relaxed coastal vibe.

highlights.name: Surf Point
highlights.description: Breaks for all levels, with surf schools nearby.

name: Talpe Beach
description: A quiet golden beach with calm natural rock pools, palm trees, and sunset views.

highlights.name: Rock Pools
highlights.description: Swimmable pools formed by coral and rocks at low tide.

name: Midigama Beach
description: Renowned for surf breaks, a chilled vibe, and picturesque sunsets.

highlights.name: Lazy Left & Right
highlights.description: Two iconic surf spots: Lazy Left for long left-handers, Lazy Right for mellow rides.

name: Koggala Beach
description: Wide sandy beach famous for stilt fishermen, lagoons, and turtle hatcheries.

highlights.name: Stilt Fishermen
highlights.description: Traditional fishermen balancing on wooden stilts along the shore.

highlights.name: Turtle Hatchery
highlights.description: See and learn about sea turtle conservation efforts.

name: Mirissa Beach
description: Famous for its crescent-shaped bay, whale watching, vibrant nightlife, and palm tree swing.

highlights.name: Whale Watching
highlights.description: Boat trips to see blue whales and dolphins (Nov–Apr).

highlights.name: Parrot Rock
highlights.description: Rocky islet offering panoramic views of the bay.

name: Hiriketiya Beach
description: Horseshoe-shaped bay with beginner-friendly surf, yoga, beach cafes, and a mellow atmosphere.

highlights.name: Surf Lessons
highlights.description: Ideal spot for learning to surf and relaxing at beachside cafes.

name: Tangalle Beach
description: A peaceful stretch of golden sand and turquoise water, ideal for relaxation and sunset views.

highlights.name: Sunset Point
highlights.description: The best spot for sunset watching and evening strolls.

name: Hummanaya Blow Hole
description: Sri Lanka’s only blowhole and the second largest in the world, shooting seawater high into the air.

highlights.name: Water Jet Viewpoint
highlights.description: Best seen during rough seas for dramatic water spouts.

name: Dikwella Beach
description: A tranquil, sandy beach popular with families and for swimming in calm waters.

highlights.name: Safe Swimming Area
highlights.description: Protected bay ideal for a relaxed swim.

name: Wewurukannala Vihara Temple
description: Features Sri Lanka’s largest seated Buddha statue, a temple with ornate murals, and quirky statues.

highlights.name: Giant Buddha Statue
highlights.description: At 50m tall, this is the tallest Buddha statue in Sri Lanka.

name: Tissamaharama Raja Maha Vihara
description: One of the largest and oldest Buddhist stupas in Sri Lanka, a place of pilgrimage with a tranquil atmosphere.

highlights.name: Historic Stupa
highlights.description: Impressive white dagoba dating back to the 2nd century BC.

name: Tissa Lake
description: A picturesque lake fringed by palm trees, great for bird watching, boat rides, and sunset walks.

highlights.name: Birdwatching Spot
highlights.description: Home to herons, egrets, pelicans, and more.

name: Sinharaja Forest Reserve
description: Sri Lanka’s last viable area of primary tropical rainforest, a UNESCO World Heritage Site renowned for endemic wildlife, bird watching, and guided rainforest walks.

highlights.name: Rainforest Trails
highlights.description: Guided nature walks through ancient forest teeming with rare birds and plants.

highlights.name: Bird Watching
highlights.description: Spotting rare endemics such as the Sri Lanka blue magpie and red-faced malkoha.

highlights.name: Waterfall Swimming
highlights.description: Cool off in natural jungle pools and hidden waterfalls.

name: Kanneliya Rainforest
description: Part of the Kanneliya–Dediyagala–Nakiyadeniya (KDN) forest complex, this rainforest is a biodiversity hotspot with streams, cascades, and endemic species.

highlights.name: Anagimale Waterfall
highlights.description: Picturesque jungle waterfall with a natural pool for swimming.

highlights.name: Canopy Walk
highlights.description: Suspension bridges and canopy walks for forest views and birdlife.

name: Makandawa Forest Reserve
description: A lush rainforest near Kitulgala, known for birding, riverine rainforest, and adventure activities like white-water rafting.

highlights.name: Jungle Bird Watching
highlights.description: Look for the rare Sri Lanka spot-winged thrush and green pit viper.

highlights.name: Rainforest Trails
highlights.description: Guided walks and nature trails under dense jungle canopy.

name: Horton Plains National Park
description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, Horton Plains is a montane cloud forest and grassland plateau, famous for its dramatic landscapes, rare flora and fauna, and stunning viewpoints.

highlights.name: World's End
highlights.description: A sheer precipice with a 900-meter drop and breathtaking views over the southern plains.

highlights.name: Baker's Falls
highlights.description: A picturesque waterfall surrounded by lush forest, popular with hikers.

highlights.name: Mini World's End
highlights.description: A smaller cliff viewpoint, less crowded, with scenic valley vistas.

highlights.name: Sambar Deer Watching
highlights.description: Early morning sightings of wild sambar deer grazing in the grasslands.

highlights.name: Cloud Forest Trails
highlights.description: Walking trails through unique highland cloud forest, rich in endemic flora and birds.

name: Bentota Beach
description: A popular golden sand beach known for safe swimming, water sports, and relaxing resorts along the southern coast.

highlights.name: Water Sports
highlights.description: Try jet-skiing, windsurfing, banana boats, and more at the Bentota Lagoon.

highlights.name: Turtle Hatchery
highlights.description: Visit nearby turtle conservation projects in Kosgoda.

name: Brief Garden
description: The former home and estate of landscape architect Bevis Bawa, now a lush and whimsical tropical garden open to visitors.

highlights.name: Garden Walks
highlights.description: Stroll winding paths through exotic trees, sculptures, and tranquil ponds.

name: Madu Ganga River Safari
description: A scenic boat safari through mangroves and islets of the Madu Ganga river, home to abundant birdlife and traditional cinnamon island villages.

highlights.name: Mangrove Forests
highlights.description: Explore winding waterways teeming with wildlife.

highlights.name: Cinnamon Island
highlights.description: Visit local families and learn about cinnamon peeling.

name: Ahungalla Beach
description: A wide and quiet sandy beach known for its palm groves, tranquil resorts, and beautiful sunsets, ideal for peaceful getaways.

highlights.name: Sunset Walks
highlights.description: Stroll along the palm-fringed shoreline as the sun sets over the Indian Ocean.
